km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard De Hywis於1150年出生在英國時，他的父親Richard De Hywis21歲，母親Mrs. William De Hywis21歲。他和Mrs. Richard De Hywis至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard De Hywis于1150年出生在英国时，他的父亲Richard De Hywis21岁，母亲Mrs. William De Hywis21岁。他和Mrs. Richard De Hywis至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hywis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 Norman, English: variant of Hawes with rounding of -a-.
2  English: sometimes perhaps a variant of   Huish  .

variants.name: Hawes
meanings: of Norman origin, from Anglo-Norman French and Middle English Haueis, Hawis, from an Old French form of the ancient Germanic female personal name Hadewidis (from had- ‘war’ + wid- with a range of possible original meanings).
variant of Haw , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Huish
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Huish (Somerset, Wiltshire, Devon, Dorset), named with Old English hīwisc ‘household, family, measure of land sufficient to support a family’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard De Hywis was born in 1150, in England, his father, Richard De Hywis, was 21 and his mother, Mrs. William De Hywis, was 21. He had at least 1 son with Mrs. Richard De Hywis.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard De Hywis est né en 1150, à Angleterre, son père, Richard De Hywis, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Mrs. William De Hywis, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs. Richard De Hywis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard De Hywis 1150 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard De Hywis 21 und seine Mutter Mrs. William De Hywis 21. Er hatte mit Mrs. Richard De Hywis mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard De Hywis è nato il nel 1150 nel England, il suo padre, Richard De Hywis, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. William De Hywis, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs. Richard De Hywis.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard De Hywisが1150年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Richard De Hywisは21歳，母Mrs. William De Hywisは21歳であった。彼はMrs. Richard De Hywisとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard De Hywis(이)가 1150년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard De Hywis(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. William De Hywis(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Mrs. Richard De Hywis(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard De Hywis nasceu em 1150, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard De Hywis, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. William De Hywis, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs. Richard De Hywis.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard De Hywis родился в 1150 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Richard De Hywis было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Mrs. William De Hywis, 21. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs. Richard De Hywis.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard De Hywis nació en 1150, en Inglaterra, su papá, Richard De Hywis, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Mrs. William De Hywis, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mrs. Richard De Hywis.
